draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners benefits from high-contrast, energetic color pops that signal urgency and performance
facade.label: Bright neon accents
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers vibrant, attention-grabbing visual language—likely drawn to modern fitness/sport aesthetics with electric blues, lime greens, or hot pinks
facade.rejectImplies: User favors muted, natural, or pastel palettes—suggests preference for calm, minimal visual language that doesn't compete with content

evidence.hypothesis: A weather app for runners benefits from high-contrast, energetic color pops that signal urgency and performance
evidence.implication: User prefers vibrant, attention-grabbing visual language—likely drawn to modern fitness/sport aesthetics with electric blues, lime greens, or hot pinks

facade.hypothesis: A clean, high-contrast design with lots of white space and clear typography appeals to runners who want quick info at a glance without distraction.
facade.axisTargeted: digital vs organic; calm vs energetic
facade.label: Bright, minimal interface
facade.acceptImplies: User favors stripped-down, functional aesthetics—likely prefers data legibility and modern simplicity over personality or warmth.
facade.rejectImplies: User may want more visual texture, personality, or atmospheric quality—perhaps more warmth, color, or organic feel to match the physical act of running.

facade.hypothesis: For a running weather app, a fixed sidebar navigation might compete with map/run data visibility, but tabbed switching preserves screen real estate and supports quick mode-switching between weather, route, and stats.
facade.axisTargeted: Layout structure: persistent sidebar vs. modal/tab-based navigation
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers always-visible navigation anchors and willing to trade horizontal space for navigation clarity and glanceability.
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes immersive content area and prefers navigation hidden or minimized to edges/gestures, accepting slower access for more map breathing room.

facade.hypothesis: Runners want to see their pace/distance milestones overlaid spatially (where the 1-mile mark actually is) rather than in a separate stats panel
facade.label: Splits and intervals on map
facade.acceptImplies: Users value geographic context for performance metrics; design should layer run data onto the route itself, not separate it into cards or panels
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer abstracted performance stats (pace, splits, time) divorced from location; a clean numerical dashboard outweighs spatial clutter

facade.hypothesis: Showing location updates as animated micro-interactions rather than instant static placement builds perceived responsiveness and maps the runner's real-time movement through playful motion instead of cold precision.
facade.axisTargeted: Motion—animated continuous feedback vs instant static updates
facade.label: GPS dot bounces in
facade.acceptImplies: User values liveliness and feels engaged by micro-motion; app should layer animated transitions throughout (location, pace changes, weather alerts).
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clarity over motion; app should snap to states instantly and reserve animation only for major state shifts (not location pings).

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners benefits from approachable, friendly design language that feels less clinical and more human, balancing the high-energy neon accents with visual warmth
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward softer, more forgiving visual language—likely wants the app to feel supportive and less aggressive, even within an energetic color palette
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers crisp, modern, athletic geometry—sharp edges align with precision and performance metrics that runners care about

facade.hypothesis: A running-focused weather app should use encouraging, energetic language and celebrate user decisions (e.g., 'Perfect tempo conditions!' vs neutral 'Wind: 8mph').
facade.label: Motivational coach tone
facade.acceptImplies: The app adopts a supportive, cheering personality that treats running as an achievement to celebrate.
facade.rejectImplies: The app maintains neutral, factual tone—data-driven and minimal on personality, letting runners form their own interpretations.

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
draft.html:

San Francisco, CA

Humidity

UV Index

58°F • Light wind
Ideal
12:00 PM
65°F • Moderate wind
Good
6:00 PM
61°F • Gusty
Fair
